circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi, was designed by Hermann Tilke and has served as the venue for Formula One's season-closing Abu Dhabi Grand Prix since 2009. The circuit represents the second Formula One facility established in the Middle East, following Bahrain and preceding later additions in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a street racing venue encircling Albert Park Lake in Melbourne, hosting the annual Formula One Australian Grand Prix alongside various support categories including the Supercars Championship round. Holding the highest FIA safety classification, the track serves as a key fixture on the international racing calendar.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located near Spielberg in Austria's Styria region, this 4. 326 km circuit originally opened as the Österreichring and served as the home of the Austrian Grand Prix throughout the 1970s and 1980s. Following reconstruction and renaming as the A1-Ring, the venue resumed hosting the Austrian Grand Prix in the late 1990s and early 2000s.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan's capital, situated alongside Baku Boulevard, and at 6. 003 km represents the fourth-longest venue on the Formula One schedule. The circuit first hosted the European Grand Prix in 2016 before becoming home to the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards, with operations managed by Baku City Circuit Operation Company.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, Bahrain International Circuit is a 5. 412 km venue that hosted the inaugural Middle Eastern Formula One race and continues to stage the annual Bahrain Grand Prix alongside other motorsport competitions including GP2 racing and endurance events. The track, which holds FIA Grade 1 status, features multiple configuration layouts and previously accommodated Australian V8 Supercars competition between 2006 and 2010.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this Spanish racing venue spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a diverse mix of straightaways and corner configurations that test all aspects of driver skill. The circuit holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstand facilities.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Located in the Walloon region of Belgium near Spa, this 7. 004 km circuit has been a cornerstone of Formula One racing since the sport's inaugural 1950 season. The venue has hosted the Belgian Grand Prix annually since 1985, with only two absences in 2003 and 2006, establishing itself as one of the championship's most enduring and storied racing facilities.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361 km temporary street circuit that serves as the home of Canada's Formula One Grand Prix. Beyond Formula One, the venue has attracted numerous international racing series including prototype and touring car championships throughout its history.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit, located in Shanghai's Jiading District, hosted the Chinese Grand Prix intermittently from 2004 until 2019, with races resuming from 2024 onwards. The venue also began accommodating the Shanghai ePrix electric racing series in 2024, establishing itself as a multi-category motorsport destination.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located near villages in Northamptonshire, England, Silverstone Circuit has been the primary home of the British Grand Prix since 1987, though it initially hosted the race in 1948. The venue holds historic significance as the site of the inaugural 1950 World Championship race, before the event alternated among three British tracks during the following three decades.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, located near Budapest in Hungary, is a 4. 381 km circuit that hosted the first Formula One Grand Prix held in Eastern Europe when it opened in 1986. Built in just eight months near a major highway after the government rejected plans for a street circuit in Budapest's central park, the track was inaugurated with a race dedicated to János Drapál, a pioneering Hungarian motorcycle racer.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Milan in northern Italy, the Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km track constructed in 1922, making it mainland Europe's oldest purpose-built racing circuit. The venue annually hosts the Italian Grand Prix, a tradition spanning since 1949 with only one interruption in 1980 for renovation work, and has earned the nickname "Temple of Speed" for its notably lengthy straights and fast-flowing corner design.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, situated in Mie Prefecture, Japan, spans 5. 807 kilometres and holds a crowd capacity of 155,000 spectators. Operated by Honda Mobilityland, the track serves as the primary venue for Formula One's Japanese Grand Prix and is renowned for hosting international motorsport racing at the highest levels of competition.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, utilizing roads adjacent to the famous Las Vegas Strip along Boulevard. The layout features several of the region's iconic landmarks, such as major hotels and entertainment venues, as part of its racing configuration.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit named in honor of two Mexican racing brothers, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both of whom died in racing accidents. The venue has hosted the Formula One Mexican Grand Prix in three distinct eras, with the current continuous period beginning in 2015 on its present layout, while previous races occurred on a different configuration ending in 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary track constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring 3. 363 miles of racing surface with 19 corners and qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph. Designed specifically by leading circuit architects for Formula One, this venue joined the championship calendar in 2022 as the home of the Miami Grand Prix.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a 3. 337 km street circuit that winds through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, following the harbour perimeter in Monaco. Though officially named after the principality, it is widely known as the Monte Carlo circuit due to its primary location within that particular district.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, Circuit Zandvoort is a 4. 259 km track that has hosted the Dutch Grand Prix since its Formula One return in 2021, though this arrangement will conclude following the 2026 season. The venue had previously been absent from the calendar before its recent revival as a championship-level racing destination.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit, situated north of Doha in Qatar, is a 5. 419 km track featuring a 1. 068 km main straight and holding FIA Grade 1 certification.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Located in São Paulo, Brazil, Interlagos is a 4. 309 km counterclockwise racing circuit that opened in 1940 and was later renamed in 1985 to commemorate the late Formula One driver José Carlos Pace. The venue hosts major motorsport events and features a karting facility dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: A street circuit running along Jeddah's waterfront beside the Red Sea, this venue has been the home of the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since its 2021 introduction to the Formula One calendar. The facility also began hosting an electric racing event starting in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit is a street-based racing venue situated around Singapore's Marina Bay, spanning the Downtown Core and Kallang urban districts.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced design combining extended straights with diverse corner configurations, making it a versatile test for all-round racing performance. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates up to 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: Located near Austin, Texas, the Circuit of the Americas is a 3. 426-mile international racing venue that serves as the permanent home of Formula One's United States Grand Prix. The track meets the highest FIA standards and accommodates multiple motorsport categories, including MotoGP and endurance racing competitions alongside its primary F1 fixture.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai and British racing driver who races for Williams in Formula One under the Thai flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso competes for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has secured two world championships (2005 and 2006 with Renault) and claimed 32 race victories over 23 seasons. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans victories with Toyota, the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, and the 2019 Daytona 24 Hours, making him the sole driver to win both F1 and World Endurance championships.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli made his Formula One debut in 2025 and currently races for Mercedes, where he has already secured five Grand Prix victories.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Haas, representing the team in the sport's top tier.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabi Bortoleto currently races in Formula One as a member of the Audi team, competing at the highest level of motorsport.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas currently races for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from over a decade in the sport. He achieved two runner-up finishes in the championship standings during his Mercedes years, 2019 and 2020, and has accumulated 10 Grand Prix victories throughout his career.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Argentine driver Franco Alejandro Colapinto currently races in Formula One as a member of the Alpine team. He has established himself as a competitor in the sport's premier racing category.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan combines endurance racing commitments in the European Le Mans Series LMP2 category with a reserve role at Haas in Formula One, having made seven Grand Prix appearances between 2024 and 2025.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The driver achieved a notable victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix during his time with AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar is a French-Algerian racing driver currently racing for Red Bull Racing in Formula One, competing under France's designation.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari, holding the distinction of seven world championship titles, which he shares with Michael Schumacher. He also leads the sport in career victories, pole positions, and podium appearances.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races for Audi in Formula One and has achieved significant success beyond the sport's premier series, including victory at the 24 Hours of Le Mans with Porsche in 2015.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: New Zealand driver Liam Lawson currently races in Formula One, representing Racing Bulls on the grid.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: The Monégasque driver currently races for Ferrari in Formula One, having secured eight Grand Prix victories throughout his nine-season career. Leclerc finished as championship runner-up in 2022, narrowly missing the title with his Italian team.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Danish driver Kevin Magnussen, known as K-Mag, raced in Formula One across two separate stints from 2014-2020 and 2022-2024. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship for BMW's factory team WRT and contests select NASCAR Cup Series events for Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: British driver Lando Norris secured the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship while racing for McLaren, adding to his career total of 11 Grand Prix victories accumulated over eight seasons in the sport.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. He previously achieved his sole Grand Prix victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: The Mexican driver competes for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from a 15-season career that includes six Grand Prix victories. Pérez finished as championship runner-up in 2023 during his time with Red Bull before his move to his current team.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri, an Australian driver competing for McLaren in Formula One, has secured nine Grand Prix victories during his four seasons in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known by the nickname "the Honey Badger," competed in Formula One over a 14-year span from 2011 through 2024, securing victories in eight races during his career in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: George Russell represents Mercedes in Formula One competition as a British driver, having claimed seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season tenure in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz Vázquez de Castro currently races for Williams in Formula One, where he has secured four race victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver who competed in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to sports car racing. He currently races in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 class with Proton Competition, having previously driven LMP2 machinery for Era Motorsport in IMSA competition.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian motorsport competitor who currently races for Aston Martin in Formula One.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver currently holding a reserve position with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls. He competed as a full-time Formula One driver from 2021 until 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: The Dutch racing driver competes for Red Bull Racing in Formula One and has secured four consecutive world championship titles between 2021 and 2024. Across his 12 seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu represents China as a reserve driver for the Cadillac Formula One team. The racing driver competed in the championship from 2022 through 2024 and holds the distinction of being the sole Chinese competitor to have participated in the sport's highest level.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team operated as Renault's works entry until the manufacturer's 2025 departure, competing since 2021 under the Alpine brand following a rebranding from its previous Renault F1 Team identity. Running Mercedes powerunits and holding a French racing licence, Alpine secured its only victory during the 2021 season when Esteban Ocon triumphed at Hungary.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has competed in Formula One through different organizational structures, with the modern incarnation operating as a British-based racing entity that campaigns under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of Italian automaker Ferrari, stands as the sport's longest-running and most victorious team, having entered every Formula One World Championship since the series began in 1950. Nicknamed after their emblematic horse crest, the squad remains an iconic fixture in motorsport history.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: An American Formula One outfit founded by NASCAR entrepreneur Gene Haas, the team entered the sport in 2016 and has been led by principal Ayao Komatsu since early 2024, succeeding the long-serving Guenther Steiner who directed operations from the team's creation through the previous season.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: A Swiss-based motorsport engineering firm established in 1970, the team distinguished itself through endurance racing success including the 1989 Le Mans 24 Hours victory before joining Formula One in 1993. Operating as an independent constructor through numerous ownership transitions, it became the sport's fourth-longest-serving team by race count before ceasing operations in 2025, with its facilities and operations subsequently forming the foundation for Audi's Formula One program.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One outfit headquartered at its technology facility in Woking. The team operates as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group, which is owned by Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has competed in Formula One continuously since 1954, operating as both a team owner and engine supplier. The current team, based in Brackley under a German racing licence, became jointly owned in 2022 by Mercedes-Benz Group, Toto Wolff, and Ineos in equal shares. The marque's racing programmes are historically associated with the "Silver Arrows" designation.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in 2024 as part of Red Bull's two-team operation. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the team operates as Red Bull's second constructor alongside Red Bull Racing, sharing resources and support from the Austrian parent company.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in the 2024 season as one of two Red Bull GmbH-owned entries. Based in Faenza with operations in Milton Keynes, the squad operates as a sister team to the primary Red Bull Racing operation.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, an Austrian-licensed team headquartered in England, competes in Formula One as one of two squads owned by Red Bull GmbH. Christian Horner led the organization from its 2005 inception through 2024, after which Laurent Mekies assumed the leadership role.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering stands as a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, succeeding Williams's earlier failed racing venture. Operating from its Grove headquarters in Oxfordshire, the team has established itself as a significant constructor in the sport's history.
